name: HEATMAP TABLE

meta.form: entities x metrics table, cells shaded per column and numbered
meta.data_required: entity rows x numeric metric columns with different scales

use_when: entities compared across metrics with different scales (progress %, punch count, tests left)
the reader looks up their row but the pattern should jump out
not_when: one shared scale across all columns (use CH-COR-03's global mode)
more than 8 rows or 6 metric columns

data_shape.title: string, action title naming the row that stands out
data_shape.eyebrow: string caps, rendered only when the DL chrome asks for it
data_shape.rows: array of {label, values[] same length as columns}

scale.note: each column normalized to its own max: intensity means 'high for this metric', not absolute size

rules: per-column normalization; the spec's one job is making mixed scales comparable
intensity encodes magnitude, not judgment: the title says whether high is good or bad
rows in the reader's natural order, never sorted by health
anti_patterns: red-green judgment coloring on magnitude data
qa: title names the standout row with a number
